candidates.why: Primary top-line revenue row; appears as the standard reported sales line.
Duplicate/alternate revenue label with identical values; useful as fallback if label changes by year.
Revenue-related but explicitly non-GAAP, so less preferred than reported revenue.

variables.notes: Use reported revenue labels as priority list; do not use Non-GAAP Revenues when GAAP/IFRS revenue rows exist.

candidates.why: Direct cost line immediately below revenue; standard COGS mapping.
Explicit alternate COGS label with identical values; useful as fallback if label varies across periods.
Component of cost of sales, but subtotal/total COGS line already exists and is preferable for this target.

variables.notes: Selected the reported COGS line and its explicit alternate label as a priority list; excluded depreciation/amortization component rows and the partial component Other Cost of sales.

candidates.why: Explicit SG&A row, populated, and preferred over underlying subcomponents per hierarchy.
Small component/subline within SG&A, not preferred when full SG&A row exists.
Component of SG&A, but full populated SG&A row exists and should be selected alone.
Possible SG&A-related component, but should not be added when explicit populated SG&A already exists.
Overhead-like expense, but much broader and likely outside SG&A-only definition; explicit SG&A row exists.

variables.notes: Explicit populated SG&A row exists, so selected only that row per hierarchy. Did not add R&D because the only R&D line is supplemental and appears below operating income, so including it in XSGA would violate the placement rule and risk double counting.

candidates.why: Only explicit R&D-labelled row present; suitable for XRD even though it appears in supplemental section below operating income.

variables.notes: Mapped to the only explicit R&D line. It appears below operating income, so it is not included in XSGA_COMPONENTS.

candidates.why: Most specific recurring interest-expense row; preferred over broader Financial expense and over totals that may include non-interest items.
Broader finance-cost row that may include fair value changes and other non-interest items; less preferred than explicit interest expense.
Recurring interest component, but likely a subset/detail of Interest expenses, so selecting both risks double counting.
Contra/adjustment to interest expense rather than expensed financing cost; not selected as standalone XINT.

variables.notes: Selected the explicit interest-expense line and avoided broader Financial expense because it appears to include non-interest finance items. Also excluded lease-interest detail and capitalized interest to avoid double counting/offset ambiguity.

candidates.why: Only clear balance-sheet equity line in candidates; use when no parent/owners-only equity line exists on the balance sheet.

variables.notes: No owner/parent-only equity line is shown on the balance sheet. Per rule, use Total Equity for BE and set MIB to [] to avoid double counting non-controlling interests.

candidates.why: Explicit NCI/minority interest line on balance sheet.
Duplicate alternate label for NCI with identical values.

variables.notes: Because BE uses Total Equity and no parent-only equity line exists on the balance sheet, MIB is set to [] to avoid double counting non-controlling interests in BE + MIB.
